coreJobStatement: Generate, distribute, and meter thermal energy (heating and cooling) from centralised plants to multiple buildings via closed-pipe networks [SRC: VN_steam_and_air_conditioning_supply]
lPath: L7 District Energy Ecosystem > L6.3 Building Substation & Energy Transfer > L5.3.3 Substation Flow & Temperature Measurement > L4.3.3.1 Ultrasonic Flow Sensor

segments.name: Residential building connection
segments.characteristics: Individual apartment building on district network; low-load, highly variable demand profile; EU EED mandates individual metering for billing
segments.targetabilityReason: billing-grade accuracy required, cost sensitivity high
segments.circumstance: Individual apartment building on district network; low-load, highly variable demand profile; EU EED mandates individual metering for billing
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — billing-grade accuracy required, cost sensitivity high

segments.name: Commercial / institutional building
segments.characteristics: Office, hospital, university on district network; higher loads, multi-zone, BMS integration; energy reporting mandated
segments.targetabilityReason: larger pipe sizes, SCADA integration, higher accuracy expectation
segments.circumstance: Office, hospital, university on district network; higher loads, multi-zone, BMS integration; energy reporting mandated
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — larger pipe sizes, SCADA integration, higher accuracy expectation

segments.name: Industrial thermal offtake
segments.characteristics: Factory or process facility drawing process heat from district network; continuous high-load, tighter temperature control
segments.targetabilityReason: higher temperatures/pressures, flow rates may exceed sensor range
segments.circumstance: Factory or process facility drawing process heat from district network; continuous high-load, tighter temperature control
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — higher temperatures/pressures, flow rates may exceed sensor range

segments.name: District cooling consumer
segments.characteristics: Data centre, shopping mall, or office in warm climate; chilled water at 5–7 °C; narrow delta-T (3–8 K) challenges meter accuracy
segments.targetabilityReason: low delta-T amplifies flow measurement error in energy calculation
segments.circumstance: Data centre, shopping mall, or office in warm climate; chilled water at 5–7 °C; narrow delta-T (3–8 K) challenges meter accuracy
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — low delta-T amplifies flow measurement error in energy calculation

segments.name: Legacy steam-to-hot-water conversion
segments.characteristics: Building previously served by steam, now converting to hot-water district network; retrofit constraints, existing pipe routing
segments.targetabilityReason: retrofit installation space constraints, mixed-age infrastructure
segments.circumstance: Building previously served by steam, now converting to hot-water district network; retrofit constraints, existing pipe routing
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — retrofit installation space constraints, mixed-age infrastructure

segments.name: 4th/5th generation low-temperature network
segments.characteristics: New low-temperature network (50–70 °C supply); prosumer buildings injecting excess heat back; bidirectional flow
segments.targetabilityReason: bidirectional flow detection essential; lower delta-T
segments.circumstance: New low-temperature network (50–70 °C supply); prosumer buildings injecting excess heat back; bidirectional flow
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — bidirectional flow detection essential; lower delta-T

alternatives.name: Mechanical Impeller / Turbine Meter (incumbent)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Moving parts subject to magnetite fouling and bearing wear; accuracy degrades from Class 2 to Class 3 within 3–5 years in hard-water networks; mandatory 5–6 year metrological re-verification cycle; pressure drop across impeller; low-flow dead band where rotor stalls [SRC: web_search — Kamstrup, KROHNE]

alternatives.name: Ultrasonic Transit-Time Flow Sensor (growing)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Accuracy degrades with >2% air entrainment; requires pipe-full condition; higher unit cost vs mechanical (1.5–2.5x); signal loss during transient air pockets; requires acoustic coupling verification at commissioning [SRC: 01_product_decomposition]

alternatives.name: Electromagnetic Flow Meter (premium)
alternatives.tradeoffs: 3–5x cost of ultrasonic; requires minimum electrical conductivity (~5 μS/cm); heavier and larger footprint; higher power consumption; typically reserved for large commercial/industrial substations (DN50+) [SRC: VN_steam_and_air_conditioning_supply]

alternatives.name: Coriolis Flow Meter (custody transfer)
alternatives.tradeoffs: 10–20x cost of ultrasonic; heavy (vibrating tubes); large footprint; overkill accuracy for building-level billing; used only in high-value custody transfer points at plant level [SRC: web_search]

alternatives.name: Do nothing / estimated allocation [Status Quo]
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero cost, zero lifecycle burden; thermal energy allocated by heated floor area or fixed percentage; cross-subsidisation between buildings; non-compliant with EU EED individual metering mandate; no fault detection; no incentive for energy saving

jobSteps.description: the required flow range, pipe diameter, medium type (hot/chilled water), temperature envelope, accuracy class (EN 1434), and communication protocol for the building substation
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Sensor specifications must match substation design and regulatory requirements
jobSteps.rawStatement: Define the required flow range, pipe diameter, medium type (hot/chilled water), temperature envelope, accuracy class (EN 1434), and communication protocol for the building substation
jobSteps.rationale: Sensor specifications must match substation design and regulatory requirements

jobSteps.description: the optimal measurement point in the substation piping where straight-run requirements are met and maintenance access is possible
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Compact substation enclosures constrain installation; position affects accuracy
jobSteps.rawStatement: Locate the optimal measurement point in the substation piping where straight-run requirements are met and maintenance access is possible
jobSteps.rationale: Compact substation enclosures constrain installation; position affects accuracy

jobSteps.description: piping section, electrical connections, and integration with energy calculator/integrator and BMS gateway
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Mechanical and electrical integration across hundreds of substations
jobSteps.rawStatement: Prepare piping section, electrical connections, and integration with energy calculator/integrator and BMS gateway
jobSteps.rationale: Mechanical and electrical integration across hundreds of substations

jobSteps.description: readiness: pipe-full condition, absence of air locks, correct orientation, communication handshake with energy calculator, MID seal integrity
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Pre-operation verification critical for billing-grade measurement
jobSteps.rawStatement: Confirm readiness: pipe-full condition, absence of air locks, correct orientation, communication handshake with energy calculator, MID seal integrity
jobSteps.rationale: Pre-operation verification critical for billing-grade measurement

jobSteps.description: continuous flow measurement feeding real-time volumetric flow rate and temperature to the energy calculator for thermal energy integration (Q = flow × Cp × ΔT)
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Core value-creating action; drives billing accuracy and network control
jobSteps.rawStatement: Execute continuous flow measurement feeding real-time volumetric flow rate and temperature to the energy calculator for thermal energy integration (Q = flow × Cp × ΔT)
jobSteps.rationale: Core value-creating action; drives billing accuracy and network control

jobSteps.description: measurement quality: signal strength, air bubble events, empty-pipe alarms, long-term calibration drift, battery status
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Fleet-wide measurement integrity affects billing revenue and compliance
jobSteps.rawStatement: Monitor measurement quality: signal strength, air bubble events, empty-pipe alarms, long-term calibration drift, battery status
jobSteps.rationale: Fleet-wide measurement integrity affects billing revenue and compliance

stakeholders.who: District energy field service technician — installs, commissions, and maintains substation metering equipment
Network operations manager — accountable for billing accuracy, EU EED compliance, and network efficiency across hundreds/thousands of substations
Building occupant / facility manager — receives thermal comfort and pays consumption-based energy bill
Meter specifier (district energy design engineer), installer, commissioning tech, calibration/verification service, disposer
Utility metering procurement manager — responsible for fleet-wide meter purchasing decisions
National metrology authority (PTB, LNMC), energy regulator, building code inspector, energy auditor
